ogTitle: Waugh Pocket vs Waverley: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Waugh Pocket and Waverley.
heading: Waugh Pocket vs Waverley — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Waugh Pocket, Queensland and Waverley, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Waverley has a lower overall climate risk score (26/100) compared to Waugh Pocket (35/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Waugh Pocket or Waverley safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Waugh Pocket has a flood risk score of 51/100, while Waverley scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Waugh Pocket scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. Waverley scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Waugh Pocket or Waverley?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Waverley has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.77% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
